items.title: Critical Remote Code Execution in Amasty Extension
items.summary: Amasty issued a massive security release for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer (CVSS 9.8) that allows remote code execution via a crafted cookie. This flaw is actively exploited in the wild, and Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Security Update Available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update addresses multiple critical, important, and moderate security vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including issues that could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and denial of service. Specific CVEs covered include SSRF (CVE-2026-34647), Incorrect Authorization (CVE-2026-34645, CVE-2026-34646), and Stored XSS (CVE-2026-34686).

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce via REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw allowing unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any Magento or Adobe Commerce store via the REST API using a polyglot technique. Attacks have been observed on a significant percentage of stores, and while Magento 2.4.9 contains a fix, older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This security update addresses multiple critical vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, gain unauthorized access to store data, and compromise customer accounts. Adobe recommends applying patches as quickly as possible to protect against potential attacks.
